title: Textile Cutting Machine Setters, Operators, and Tenders
description: Set up, operate, or tend machines that cut textiles.

tasks.text: Inspect products to ensure that the quality standards and specifications are met.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Place patterns on top of layers of fabric and cut fabric following patterns, using electric or manual knives, cutters, or computer numerically controlled cutting devices.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Start machines, monitor operations, and make adjustments as needed.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Adjust machine controls, such as heating mechanisms, tensions, or speeds, to produce specified products.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Record information about work completed and machine settings.
tasks.rationale: Routine cognitive or manual task with high vulnerability to automation.
Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Inspect machinery to determine whether repairs are needed.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Confer with coworkers to obtain information about orders, processes, or problems.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Repair or replace worn or defective parts or components, using hand tools.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Clean, oil, and lubricate machines, using air hoses, cleaning solutions, rags, oilcans, and grease guns.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Thread yarn, thread, or fabric through guides, needles, and rollers of machines.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Operate machines to cut multiple layers of fabric into parts for articles such as canvas goods, house furnishings, garments, hats, or stuffed toys.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Adjust cutting techniques to types of fabrics and styles of garments.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.
Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Study guides, samples, charts, and specification sheets or confer with supervisors or engineering staff to determine set-up requirements.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Stop machines when specified amounts of product have been produced.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Operate machines for test runs to verify adjustments and to obtain product samples.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Install, level, and align components, such as gears, chains, guides, dies, cutters, or needles, to set up machinery for operation.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.
